title: Filtering subitems by person - says released but not working
excerpt: The board for Subitem work being done shows the subitem filtering is done. However, when I am on a board and filter by person, only the main task filters by person. All subitems attached to that task show regardless of w…

posts.author: LegalPM (Wanda)
posts.cookedHtml: The board for Subitem work being done shows the subitem filtering is done. However, when I am on a board and filter by person, only the main task filters by person. All subitems attached to that task show regardless of who it is assigned to. Will you be updating this?

posts.author: PierreM (PB)
posts.cookedHtml: I’d like to have an update on this please

posts.author: mw01 (MW)
posts.cookedHtml: Can you mirror the person column from subitems to the main item? You should be able to filter at that point. Not sure if this helps.

posts.author: PierreM (PB)
posts.cookedHtml: The problem with this is that I always more than one person assigned to all the subitems of a main item, so if I filter this way I can only have a global vision on what are the subjects the collaborators work on, not precisely the tasks they are assigned to

posts.author: sryaegle (Savanna )
posts.cookedHtml: Any updates on this @monday-team!?

posts.author: gbarker121 (Geoffrey Barker)
posts.cookedHtml: Would really help reduce screen clutter and keep people focused.

posts.author: Josephine (Josephine)
posts.cookedHtml: Anyone found a way to filter by the person column located subitems? When I try to apply this filter in a chart, it captures all subitems connected to the parent item even if they do not have the assigned person attached to it.

posts.author: jtitus (Joshua Titus)
posts.cookedHtml: Bumping this issue. It’s really debilitating when we’re trying to assign sub-items to multiple different people. Their personal dashboards are flooded with all subitems just because they’re tagged on one. Would love a solve!

posts.author: osaisacson (Osa Isacson)
posts.cookedHtml: Hear hear on the above. We really need to be able to filter subitems by person.

posts.author: anthonywoods (Anthony Woods)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi,

We are also having a similar problem with the sub items and filtering by person. When we filter by person, the first row with the sub item filters fine, but the second (and onwards) rows do not filter by the person. I don’t think this feature is working correctly and it’s really important for our sub items.

Any help would be much appreciated.

posts.author: LKat (Laura Katterhenry)
posts.cookedHtml: The ability to filter by subitem is a feature that monday.com is working on, so you won’t be able to use it yet. They said it’s being released in Q3 of this year (2022).

posts.author: mmaggart (Marty Maggart)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi, is there any update on filtering by subitem?

posts.author: KenanSeba (Kenan)
posts.cookedHtml:
It’s been months since the announcement !
Any updates on this @monday-team ?

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi all!

We sincerely apologise for the delay in responding to this thread! We are working on improving our responsiveness to the community, particularly our feature request process so appreciate your patience in advance!

On that note, if you haven’t noticed already, you should now be able to utilise advanced filters to filter by subitems in both boards and dashboards Let me know if you have any comments or feedback!

posts.author: LSC (Lori Straus)
posts.cookedHtml: I’m running into the reverse problem: when I filter by name, the sub-items for the item are included, even if they’re assigned to someone else. Any solutions for this so far?

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Lori!

Are you able to send me a screenshot of your filter settings, as well as what appears in the board once you filter?

posts.author: SarhM (Sarah Margolis)
posts.cookedHtml: I’m having trouble when I filter by assignee on workload, more people than those selected are brought into the view, ends up defeating the point of the filter

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Sarah,

This might be due to the fact that more than 1 person is assigned to the item - is this the case on your end? If not, would you be happy to share a screenshot so I can take a closer look at your set up and what might be happening?

posts.author: SarhM (Sarah Margolis)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Bianca,

Please see the screenshots at the same time.

The first is the workload view – We have multiple boards connected and have filtered on based on sub item Assignee. Chaitanya Kumar (whose workload is open) has NOT been selected in the assignee filter for the South Yorkshire board (only Benedikt Stranak has).

image001.png1053×728 43.7 KB

The second screenshot is of the South Yorkshire board, with the relevant project expanded. You can see that Chaitanya is the sponsor of the project, and then you can see where Benedikt (BS) has been selected in the sub-item Assignee column

image002.png924×892 137 KB

It would be great to hear your thoughts

Thanks,

Sarah

posts.author: SarhM (Sarah Margolis)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi,

Do you have an update on the below please?

Thanks

Sarah

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Sarah,

My apologies for the delayed response! I have raised this with our product team and will get back to you as soon as I can regarding this behaviour

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml: https://monday.slack.com/archives/C04V58RPR5Z/p1709760389907189

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Sarah,

Thank you for your patience with me!

I’ve heard back from our team and they’ve requested for this situation to instead use the quick filters.

The updated People filters works for the quick person filter , but not the regular widget filters (advanced, etc). So for example in this case, I have Pam Beesley, a Guest, assigned to a subitem on one board. On the first screenshot I’m using the quick person filter, on the second I’m using the regular filters:

Screenshot 2024-03-07 at 14.29.372502×1140 208 KB

Screenshot 2024-03-07 at 14.30.412514×1194 240 KB

Please let me know if this helps!

posts.author: SarhM (Sarah Margolis)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi,

Thanks for responding. However, that view only provides one person at a time whereas we would like to view multiple people so we can see how work is split between a team. This is why I’m using the advanced filters.

Is there another fix that utilises the filters provided?

Thanks,

Sarah

posts.author: SarhM (Sarah Margolis)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @BiancaT,

Did you find out if there are any other solutions?

Thanks

Sarah
